items.front: How are ATIS code letters handled?
items.back: Identify each message with the phonetic code at the beginning and end, use the letters sequentially Alpha through Zulu, and restart with Alpha or the assigned first letter after an interruption over 12 hours.

items.front: When must a new ATIS recording be made, and what if a pilot cannot receive ATIS?
items.back: Record a new ATIS for every new official weather report and any pertinent operational change. If a pilot cannot receive it, issue the weather, runways, approach or departure information, NOTAMs, and airport conditions.

items.front: What core information belongs in an ATIS broadcast?
items.back: Include facility name, code letter, weather time, weather and altimeter, approach and runway in use, plus pertinent advisories such as threats, closures, runway conditions, wind shear, and acknowledgment instructions.
